content:
🔥 Example: Whiplash
• Andrew wants to be one of the greatest drummers of all time.
• Fletcher pushes him beyond his limits, using abuse as his teaching method.
• Andrew has to decide how much suffering he’s willing to endure for greatness.

This push-and-pull is what makes a story engaging.

1.2 The Iceberg Method: Writing Beyond the Page

Most of what makes a story feel real is never explicitly stated in the final text. Think of writing like an iceberg:
• The top (10%) = What the audience sees—the polished, final graphic novel.
• The massive bottom (90%) = The unseen research, character work, thematic exploration, backstory that shapes every decision in the story.

Example: Tarantino writes volumes of backstory for his characters that never appear in the script, but it helps him write with confidence and depth.

💡 Your takeaway:
The more you know about your world and characters, the more natural your writing will feel.
